task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Apparel' tax class has been created without removing existing tax classes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the WooCommerce Settings > Tax tab, 'Apparel rates' should be visible as a link/sub-tab at the top of the settings area alongside 'Standard rates' and any other default rates.

task_completion.description: Verify the tax rate is configured for New York at 4.0% with the correct name.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Apparel rates' table, there should be a row with Country Code 'US' (or blank if applied to all US), State Code 'NY', Rate % '4.0000' (or '4.0'/'4'), and Tax Name 'NY Apparel Tax'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the tax rate includes shipping.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'Apparel rates' table, the 'Shipping' checkbox for the 'NY Apparel Tax' row must be checked.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Organic Cotton T-Shirt' product is assigned to the 'Apparel' tax class.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the Edit Product page for 'Organic Cotton T-Shirt', under the 'Product data' section (General tab), the 'Tax class' dropdown menu must be set to 'Apparel'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the WordPress/WooCommerce graphical user interface to make changes.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the WordPress admin dashboard (wp-admin). There should be no terminal windows, database clients (like phpMyAdmin), or WP-CLI commands used to complete the task.

integrity.description: Verify the agent navigated through the actual application menus.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent interacting with WooCommerce Settings, the Tax tab, and the Products list/edit pages, rather than just displaying a text editor or a single final state.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the visual output.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser developer tools (Inspect Element) should not be open and actively modifying the DOM text to display 'Apparel' or 'NY Apparel Tax'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target product: 'Organic Cotton T-Shirt'. Expected tax class name: 'Apparel' (slug: 'apparel'). Expected tax rate: 4.0000 (4%). Expected state code: 'NY'. Expected country code: 'US'. Expected tax name: 'NY Apparel Tax'. The tax rate must have the shipping option enabled.
